features.description: Opens the book with the running example and names the through-line. Audience touchpoint: the moment a reader picks this up — what changed in their world that made them open it.
acceptance_criteria: Opens with a one-paragraph hook that introduces the running example.
Names the through-line explicitly in the close.
review_criteria: No jargon introduced without a one-line definition.

features.description: Develops the core idea announced in chapter 1. Uses the same running example and adds a second concrete case to show the idea generalising.
acceptance_criteria: Reuses the running example from ch-introduction.
Closes with one reflection question for the reader.
review_criteria: No new running example introduced (this chapter develops, not pivots).

features.description: Walks the reader through applying the core idea to a problem of their own. Includes a 5-step exercise the reader can do in 20 minutes.
acceptance_criteria: Each step has a one-sentence why-this-step explanation.
Closes with a takeaway the reader can quote at someone.
review_criteria: No code examples (this is a non-coding pipeline).
